---
title: "2cHW4"
author: "Averey Copeland"
date: "2026-02-11"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}
pb <- 1/10
ps <- 9/10
P <- matrix(0, nrow = 6, ncol = 6)
#Manually entered values to create matrix
cat(choose(2,1)*(ps^1)*(pb^(1)))

P[1,]=c(0,0,0,0,0,1)
P[2,]=c(1/10,9/10,0,0,0,0)
P[3,]=c(1/100, .18 ,81/100,0,0,0)
P[4,]=c(1/1000,0.02700, 0.2430, 0.7290, 0,0)
P[5,]=c(1/10000, 0.00360, 0.0486, 0.2916, 0.65610,0)
P[6,]=c(1/100000, 0.00045, 0.0081, 0.0729, 0.32805, 0.59049)

#UNSURE HOW TO SOLVE B

#C
#TRANSPOSE EIGENVECTOR SO IT SOLVES PI^T IS EIG VECTOR OF P^T THAT EQUALS 1
e <- eigen(t(P))
#Normalize
pi <- eig$vectors[,1] / sum(eig$vectors[,1])

#Exactly 1 works (second pi value)
pi[2]  

```
